column5: Display Desc

A 'rest' is counted when a unit uses a Move in which they stand still, or when a unit ends their turn while still having AP. Some statuses and tile effects only trigger on each rest, like [poison] tiles.

Linear ground-based movement from one adjacent tile to another. Standard 'walking' movement is travel, but jumping/teleporting are not. Travel incurs things like [ice] tile slipperiness, while teleporting on to an ice tile would not.

Some minor actions/penalties cause Action Point crystals to receive a fracture line, but not immediately break (ie. AP is not expended). AP crystals can be fractured twice safely, but on their 3rd fracture, break and are expended. This makes some minor actions, such as standard unit 'walking' movement, 'free' or very low-cost. [Elec] damage always causes AP fracture.
Shields lessen incoming damage by the total amount of shields a unit has, unless the incoming attack is [piercing] or [bypassing].
Damage which will bypass 1 layer of [shield].
Damage which will bypass *all* layers of [shield].

A type of [travel] movement which causes damage when it *cannot* be fully completed. If a unit would be shoved 2 tiles away but a rock is in the way, the 2 tiles of motion are received as 2 damage instead. Not all externally-caused movement is knockback; usually knockback is only applied if the movement is violent or abrupt.

Reactions are actions a unit takes automatically when certain conditions are met, whether it's that unit's turn or not. Every reaction is different and has its own trigger for what causes it, such as the Knight's 'Counter Stance' move reacting to being hit with melee damage.
Units that are hovering in the air are not at all affected by tile types, nor take [ground] attack damage/effects.
Units that are too light to be affected by certain ground effects, like [ice] tiles, but are easily move by winds, like [air tunnels].
Units that cannot be easily moved by others. Notably, a heavy unit on an [ice] tile behaves like a standard non-heavy unit.
Woodland units bypass the entry penalty of [overgrowth] tiles, but still receive the benefits.
Units of immense physical force; their attacks are always [bludgeoning]. Smashers also treat [jagged] tiles as standard, neither being penalized nor resetting it.
Sprout damage always turns any impacted tiles into [overgrowth].
Water moves will always break 1/4 of a [shield] on impact, even when dealing 0 damage. Cools & erases [ember] tiles.
Ice attacks always cause the [Frigid] status to impacted units. If an ice attack hits an [ember] tile, it will convert the tile to [puddle].

Fire damage is always [piercing]. Fire also does 1 extra damage to units on [overgrowth] tiles and burns it away, or converts [ice] tiles to [puddles]. Units already on a [puddle] tile will receive -1 damage, and the [puddle] will evaporate.
Poison attacks always cause the [Poisoned] status to impacted units.
Ground attacks do not affect [hovering] units; they commonly also ignore cover and line of sight.
Always turns the tile below the impacted unit to [static]. If the unit was on a [puddle] tile, does +1 damage to them first.
Bludgeoning damage always expends 1 AP of the impacted unit.
Bleed damage turns the tile below the impacted unit to [blood].
Enchanted tiles can never be altered into a different tile type.
Jagged tiles deal 1 damage and penalize 1 AP upon a unit entering them, after which they revert to a standard tile. [Smasher] units treat these like regular tiles.
Overgrowth tiles cost 1 AP to enter (unless the unit is [woodland]), but lessens incoming damage by 1 while within it. Receives +1 extra damage and is destroyed by [fire] attacks.
Ending your turn on an ember tile incurs 1 [fire] damage, but *starting* your turn on an ember tile confers +1 AP.
Both entering and [resting] on a poison tile causes 1/4 [shield-bypassing] damage.
Slippery! Any unit that ends an action/movement on an ice tile via [travel] will slide to the far side of it, if possible. [Lightweight] units are too light to be affected.
Puddle tiles will break 1/4 [shield] each time a unit [rests] in it. Units in puddles also receive +1 damage from [elec] attacks, and -1 from [fire] attacks.
Static tiles lock the joints of units on them. Units must [rest] to clear the static, and cannot leave the tile or use non-[rest] actions until they have done so.

On its own, a blood tile has no effect; however, certain powerful moves require blood tiles to be usable. Typically, any unit capable of dealing [bleed] damage has moves taking advantage of blood tiles.

When standard-weight units [rest] in a wind tunnel, they are moved off of it in a specific, fixed direction. [Heavy] units are unaffected, while [lightweight] units are moved *immediately* upon entering the tile.
When a unit is [rests] next to an unoccupied lodestone tile, it is pulled onto it. This effect is resisted if the unit is next to multiple lodestones at once, or already on a lodestone.
